---
title: "Week 6 — Confounding and multivariable thinking"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

What else could explain the relationship?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Confounding and lurking variables; the difference between crude and
adjusted comparisons.

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

Compare a crude difference to a stratified one in a small two-by-two
table.

### Friday — quiz / application

Quiz mirroring Wednesday's micro-lab; short Simpson's-paradox case
prompt.

## Primary source basis

Harvested from OpenIntro IMS Ch 1–3 examples and selected Ch 8 prose;
no single IMS chapter is dedicated to this unit.

## Supplemental source / custom needs

Custom adjusted-vs-unadjusted cases; ISLBS clinical confounding
examples once mirrored.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Explain confounding in plain language.
- [ ] Compare crude and stratified results.
- [ ] Describe why an observed association may be misleading.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Micro-lab ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
